title: Broken tracking of symbol entries when inheriting classes
body:
Consider the following example:

```systemverilog
class Foo;
endclass

class Bar extends Foo;
   int m_field = get_1();

   function int get_1();
      return 1;
   endfunction
endclass
```

This produces the following error:
```
%Error: call_method_declared_after.sv:5:18: Can't find definition of task/function: 'get_1'
    5 |    int m_field = get_1();
      |                  ^~~~~
```

When I remove `extends Foo` from the code, or swap the order of definitions of `m_field` and `get_1`, the example passes just fine.

I'm using the latest version of Verilator under Linux (Gentoo):
```
Verilator 5.007 devel rev v5.006-120-gfaf3804b9
```
